name: DomainTools Iris Investigate
description: This app supports investigative actions to profile domain names, get risk scores, and find connected domains that share the same Whois details, web hosting profiles, SSL certificates, and more on DomainTools Iris Investigate

actions.name: Domain Reputation
actions.description: Evaluates the risk of a given domain

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Domain or comma-separated list of domains to query

actions.name: Pivot Action
actions.description: Find domains connected by any supported Iris Investigate search parameter

parameters.name: query_value
parameters.description: Value to query

parameters.name: pivot_type
parameters.description: Field to pivot on

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Return domains of this registration type

parameters.name: data_updated_after
parameters.description: Iris Investigate records that were updated on or after midnight on this date, in YYYY-MM-DD format or relative options ( 'today', 'yesterday' )

parameters.name: tld
parameters.description: Limit results to only include domains in a specific top-level domain (i.e. “tld=com” or “tld=ru”)

parameters.name: create_date
parameters.description: Only include domains created on a specific date, in YYYY-MM-DD format or relative options ( 'today', 'yesterday' )

parameters.name: create_date_within
parameters.description: Only include domains with a whois create date within the specified number of days (e.g. specifying '1' would indicate within the past day)

parameters.name: first_seen_within
parameters.description: Only include domains with a current lifecycle first observed within the specified number of seconds (e.g. specifying '86400' would indicate within the past day)

parameters.name: first_seen_since
parameters.description: Only include domains with a current lifecycle first observed since a specified datetime. (Example: 2023-04-10T00:00:00+00:00)

parameters.name: expiration_date
parameters.description: Only include domains expiring on a specific date, in YYYY-MM-DD format or relative options ( 'today', 'yesterday' )

actions.name: Reverse Domain
actions.description: Extract IPs from a single domain response for further pivoting

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Domain or comma-separated list of domains to query

actions.name: Reverse Ip
actions.description: Find domains with web hosting IP, NS IP or MX IP

parameters.name: ip
parameters.description: IP address to query

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Return domains of this registration type

parameters.name: data_updated_after
parameters.description: Iris Investigate records that were updated on or after midnight on this date, in YYYY-MM-DD format or relative options ( 'today', 'yesterday' )

parameters.name: tld
parameters.description: Limit results to only include domains in a specific top-level domain (i.e. “tld=com” or “tld=ru”)

parameters.name: create_date
parameters.description: Only include domains created on a specific date, in YYYY-MM-DD format or relative options ( 'today', 'yesterday' )

parameters.name: create_date_within
parameters.description: Only include domains with a whois create date within the specified number of days (e.g. specifying '1' would indicate within the past day)

parameters.name: first_seen_within
parameters.description: Only include domains with a current lifecycle first observed within the specified number of seconds (e.g. specifying '86400' would indicate within the past day)

parameters.name: first_seen_since
parameters.description: Only include domains with a current lifecycle first observed since a specified datetime. (Example: 2023-04-10T00:00:00+00:00)

parameters.name: expiration_date
parameters.description: Only include domains expiring on a specific date, in YYYY-MM-DD format or relative options ( 'today', 'yesterday' )

actions.name: Load Hash
actions.description: Load or monitor Iris Investigate search results by Iris Investigate export hash

parameters.name: search_hash
parameters.description: Paste the "Current Search Export" string (Advanced -> Import/Export Search) from Iris Investigate in this field to import up to 5000 domains

actions.name: Reverse Email
actions.description: Find domains with email in Whois, DNS SOA or SSL certificate

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email query

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Return domains of this registration type

parameters.name: data_updated_after
parameters.description: Iris Investigate records that were updated on or after midnight on this date, in YYYY-MM-DD format or relative options ( 'today', 'yesterday' )

parameters.name: tld
parameters.description: Limit results to only include domains in a specific top-level domain (i.e. “tld=com” or “tld=ru”)

parameters.name: create_date
parameters.description: Only include domains created on a specific date, in YYYY-MM-DD format or relative options ( 'today', 'yesterday' )

parameters.name: create_date_within
parameters.description: Only include domains with a whois create date within the specified number of days (e.g. specifying '1' would indicate within the past day)

parameters.name: first_seen_within
parameters.description: Only include domains with a current lifecycle first observed within the specified number of seconds (e.g. specifying '86400' would indicate within the past day)

parameters.name: first_seen_since
parameters.description: Only include domains with a current lifecycle first observed since a specified datetime. (Example: 2023-04-10T00:00:00+00:00)

parameters.name: expiration_date
parameters.description: Only include domains expiring on a specific date, in YYYY-MM-DD format or relative options ( 'today', 'yesterday' )

actions.name: Lookup Domain
actions.description: Get all Iris Investigate data for a domain using the Iris Investigate API endpoint (required)

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Domain or comma-separated list of domains to query using the Iris Investigate API

actions.name: Enrich Domain
actions.description: Get all Iris Investigate data for a domain except counts using the high volume Iris Enrich API endpoint (if provisioned)

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Domain or comma-separated list of domains to query using the Iris Enrich API (if provisioned)

actions.name: Configure Scheduled Playbooks
actions.description: Run on initial setup to configure the optional monitoring playbooks. This action creates a custom list to manage the playbook scheduling and run status

actions.name: On Poll
actions.description: Execute scheduled playbooks based on the set interval(mins) in 'domaintools_scheduled_playbooks' custom list. Smaller intervals will result in more accurate schedules

actions.name: Parsed Domain Rdap
actions.description: The Parsed Domain RDAP API returns the most recent Domain-RDAP registration record in response to a HTTP GET query. This API compliments the Parsed Whois API as some registries and registrar are beginning to support RDAP as an alternative to Whois for providing domain registration data

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: The domain name to query (e.g., example.com)

actions.name: Nod Feed
actions.description: Apex-level domains (e.g. example.com but not www.example.com) observed for the first time by the DomainTools sensor network, and which are not present in our DNSDB historical database

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Filter for an exact domain or a substring contained within a domain by prefixing or suffixing your substring with '*'. Check the documentation for examples

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: The end of the query window in seconds or in ISO8601 format, relative to the current time, inclusive.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Filter for records before the given time value inclusive or time offset relative to now.

parameters.name: session_id
parameters.description: A custom string to distinguish between different sessions

parameters.name: top
parameters.description: Limit the number of results to the top N, where N is the value of this parameter

actions.name: Nad Feed
actions.description: Apex-level domains (e.g. example.com but not www.example.com) DomainTools has newly observed in our DNS sensor network. This includes domains observed in DNS for the first time as well as domains observed in DNS again after not being observed for at least 10 days

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Filter for an exact domain or a substring contained within a domain by prefixing or suffixing your substring with '*'. Check the documentation for examples

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: The end of the query window in seconds or in ISO8601 format, relative to the current time, inclusive.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Filter for records before the given time value inclusive or time offset relative to now.

parameters.name: session_id
parameters.description: A custom string to distinguish between different sessions

parameters.name: top
parameters.description: Limit the number of results to the top N, where N is the value of this parameter

actions.name: Noh Feed
actions.description: Contains fully qualified domain names (i.e. host names) that have never been seen before in passive DNS, emitted as soon as they are first observed. Hostname resolutions that we observe for the first time with our global DNS sensor network

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Used to filter feed results. The filter can be an exact match or a partial match when the * character is included at the beginning and/or end of the value

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: The end of the query window in seconds or in ISO8601 format, relative to the current time, inclusive.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: The start of the query window in seconds in ISO8601 format, relative to the current time, inclusive.

parameters.name: session_id
parameters.description: Serves as a unique identifier for the session. This parameter ensures that data retrieval begins from the latest timestamp recorded in the previous data pull.

parameters.name: top
parameters.description: The number of results to return in the response payload. Primarily used for testing.

actions.name: Domain Discovery Feed
actions.description: New domains as they are either discovered in domain registration information, observed by our global sensor network, or reported by trusted third parties

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Used to filter feed results. The filter can be an exact match or a partial match when the * character is included at the beginning and/or end of the value.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: The end of the query window in seconds or in ISO8601 format, relative to the current time, inclusive.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: The start of the query window in seconds in ISO8601 format, relative to the current time, inclusive.

parameters.name: session_id
parameters.description: Serves as a unique identifier for the session. This parameter ensures that data retrieval begins from the latest timestamp recorded in the previous data pull.

parameters.name: top
parameters.description: The number of results to return in the response payload. Primarily used for testing.

actions.name: Domain Rdap Feed
actions.description: List of records for a given domain may be provided by a domain registry, registrar, or both. Domain registries maintain authoritative information about one or more top-level domains (e.g., .com), while domain registrars manage apex domains (e.g., domaintools.com). When domain information is present from both the registry and registrar, this API presents a record containing both sets of results, as well the original raw JSON record, from both the registry and registrar

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Used to filter feed results. The filter can be an exact match or a partial match when the * character is included at the beginning and/or end of the value.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: The end of the query window in seconds or in ISO8601 format, relative to the current time, inclusive.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: The start of the query window in seconds in ISO8601 format, relative to the current time, inclusive.

parameters.name: session_id
parameters.description: Serves as a unique identifier for the session. This parameter ensures that data retrieval begins from the latest timestamp recorded in the previous data pull.

parameters.name: top
parameters.description: The number of results to return in the response payload. Primarily used for testing.
